script.dialogue: Something smells nice… Who could possibly be cooking this late into the evening, though?
♪ My witch’s cauldron, full of stew… The meat and veggies start to brew… A delicious meal for me and you! ♪
Ah, that’s Annette. Did she lose track of time in the library and miss dinner again?
♪ Hm hm hm… Witch’s brew, witch’s stew… ♪
Hearing her sing always bring a smile to my face. I wonder what she’s making.
♪ A delicious meal for— ♪ Oh no! The pot’s boiling over! Darn it, Annette—
Uh-oh. That doesn’t sound good. I better check on her.
A short while later…
I… I think we saved it, somehow!
Did we? Half of it appears to be on the floor.
And half is still in the pot! That’s better than none, right?
Haha. I appreciate your ability to look on the bright side, Annette.
Though I’m surprised they still let you in here after the messes you caused recently. I was banned from kitchen duty ages ago for botching a simple pasta recipe.
So that rumor I heard was true? I was so sure someone had made it up—I can hardly picture you messing up at anything.
Oh, really? I suppose I should be flattered, but…

I really mean it! You always carry yourself with so much poise. You sing for one of the kids in town and suddenly you’re putting on a show. I sing a silly little tune while I’m cooking and get totally distracted… And then lose half a pot of stew.

Don’t be so hard on yourself, Annette. There are plenty of things I struggle with—too many to count, actually. But as a commoner, no one at the Officers Academy will take me seriously if I don’t make a good impression. So I’ve put a lot of hard work into looking… Poised, as you put it.
Huh. I hadn’t thought about it that way. It makes sense now that you’ve said it, though. That must be stressful.
It can be. But it can also be fun—like dressing up or dancing.
Those things are pretty fun.
Yeah? If you want, maybe I could give you some pointers on how to act more graceful. I’m sure you’d pick it up in no time.
I do love learning new things. Consider me interested!
Wonderful. Though if you ask me, I think you’re perfectly charming the way you are right now.
Oh, stop! You’re just saying that… Or is this part of my first lesson?
Dorothea and Annette have reached Support Rank B.
